ksa_text: Knowledge of software version control systems and Git workflows, including branching strategies, merge and rebase operations, conflict resolution, pull request processes, and collaborative development practices.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Technical Foundations KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.424) to justify inclusion for the Director of Data Governance. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.challenge: This Knowledge KSA was excluded as an edge case, yet it has a composite score of 0.424 (moderate signal). Knowledge-dimension KSAs represent conceptual awareness rather than execution; exclusion may be too aggressive.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.rebuttal: Edge-domain Knowledge KSAs require more than moderate signal to justify inclusion; the role's scope does not regularly engage with Technical Foundations. Exclusion upheld.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: Upheld: edge-domain exclusion maintained. Technical Foundations is not sufficiently central to the Director of Data Governance to warrant inclusion at this signal level.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world Director of Data Governance roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Recent job postings for Director of Data Governance roles increasingly include Technical Foundations competencies as organizations broaden role scope. External evidence supports inclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Overturn: evolving market expectations for Director of Data Governance now include Technical Foundations competencies. Revised to include.

decision_rationale: Initially excluded but overturned through adversarial review. Technical Foundations knowledge KSA is relevant to the Director of Data Governance based on external evidence and internal consistency re-evaluation.
